results.prompt: Is there anything urgent in my inbox?

score.finalText:
Yes. The only thing that looks time-sensitive is **Union Collective**.

- **Ellis O'Connell at Viox** followed up at **12:31 PM UTC** asking for movement on the **corrected title commitment**.
- That thread got internal attention right away:
  - **Jerome Daksiewicz** sent a design/layout question at **12:46 PM**
  - **Doug Franklin** said at **12:50 PM** they’d **review with the owner today**
  - **Andrew Cannon** replied to Ellis at **12:58 PM** saying he’d send comments to the owner and get back to him

What that means: **it’s active, but not dropped.** The outside party has already nudged the team, and your group is responding. I’d still keep an eye on it because it now depends on getting owner feedback back out quickly.

Second item worth your attention:
- **Quotes for Maryland** at **11:44 AM UTC** from **Brian Shows**. He says the quote/drawing shows a **new bathroom at the north end**, but he **needs an updated drawing** because he can only see a men’s room.

What that means: **this is probably blocked until someone sends corrected drawings.** That’s a cleaner action item than an emergency, but it can stall pricing or coordination if it sits.

Everything else in the batch looks non-urgent:
- Best Buy/package notifications
- Capital One card shipment notice
- Internal scheduling/follow-up email from Maria
- Niemann Carmel build-out note with photos, useful but not urgent

My read: **nothing looks like a fire in your inbox right now, but Maryland has a real blocker and Union Collective has an owner-response clock running.**

Next move:
- **Reply on Maryland now** or get the updated drawing sent.
- **Check whether Union Collective owner comments went out today.**

If you want, I can **draft the reply for the Maryland email** or **pull the Union Collective thread and draft a response**.

results.prompt: What came in through Outlook today that needs my attention?

observations: tools fired: intentPlanner, emailActionIntentRouter, mcpToolDiscoverySkipped, streamingToolPolicy, getRecentOutlookEmails
score.finalText:
What needs your attention today is mostly **Union Collective**. That’s where the live back-and-forth is, and there are two items that look like actual decision/follow-up pressure.

### Needs attention

- **Union Collective — corrected title commitment follow-up**
  - **Email from Ellis O'Connell on 2026-05-14 12:31 UTC**
  - He asked whether there’s been any movement on providing a **corrected title commitment**.
  - Andrew replied he’d send comments to the owner and get back to them, which means the ball is still moving but **this is still open**.
  - Why it matters: outside party is chasing status, so if the owner hasn’t responded yet, this can stall survey/title-related progress.

- **Union Collective — layout / cooler space decision**
  - **Email from Jerome Daksiewicz on 2026-05-14 12:46 UTC**
  - Jerome asked about **moving the walk-ins next to the vendor stalls** to eliminate guest/BOH cross-traffic.
  - Doug responded he liked the idea and said **they’d review it with the owner today on the call**.
  - Andrew then asked **how much cooler space would be lost**.
  - Why it matters: this is a real design decision, not chatter. It needs an owner call outcome or a quantified answer on the space tradeoff.

### Lower-priority but worth knowing

- **Quotes for Maryland**
  - **Email from Brian Shows on 2026-05-14 11:44 UTC**
  - He said the quote and drawing show a **new bathroom at the north end**, but they need an **updated drawing** because he can only see a men’s room.
  - That looks like a coordination gap. If you’re waiting on pricing or vendor alignment, this will hold it up.

- **Niemann Carmel Build Out**
  - **Email from Douglas Franklin on 2026-05-14 10:48 UTC**
  - Doug shared existing condition photos for the buildout space.
  - Useful, but not urgent unless you’re actively pushing that job into setup.

### Ignore / admin noise

- Best Buy shipping notices
- PC Heaven marketplace messages
- Capital One card shipped notice
- Maria’s scheduling follow-up unless you specifically need to step into that meeting setup

### Bottom line

The real pressure today is **Union Collective**:
1. **Get clarity on the corrected title commitment**
2. **Close the loop on the walk-in relocation idea and cooler space impact**

If you want, I can draft:
- a reply on the **title commitment follow-up**, or
- a short internal/outbound reply on the **Union Collective layout question**.

Two from today look like they need attention.

1. **Union Collective (Union, KY)** — email from **Ellis O'Connell** on **2026-05-14 12:31 UTC**
   - Subject: **RE: Union Collective (Union, KY)**
   - Why it matters: Ellis is **following up** asking whether there has been any movement on the **corrected title commitment**.
   - Current signal: Andrew replied at 12:58 UTC saying he’d send Ellis’s comments to the owner and get back to him. So the thread has been acknowledged, but it still likely needs a substantive follow-up once the owner responds.

2. **Quotes for Maryland** — email from **Brian Shows** on **2026-05-14 11:44 UTC**
   - Subject: **RE: Quotes for Maryland**
   - Why it matters: Brian says the **quote and drawing don’t align** — the drawing only shows a men’s room, but they need an **updated drawing** for the new bathroom at the north end.
   - This looks like a real blocker and likely needs a reply or forwarded action today.

Everything else in today’s inbox sample looks informational, internal, or low-value for reply:
- package/shipping notices
- Capital One notification
- internal FYI on Niemann Carmel Build Out
- Maria’s external scheduling email appears to be outbound, not something waiting on you
- the Union Collective development-regulations thread appears actively handled internally already

The point: **“Quotes for Maryland” is the one I’d treat as the real reply-needed item right now.** The Union Collective title commitment thread is the other one to keep moving if owner input hasn’t come back yet.

If you want, I can draft the reply to **Brian Shows** now, or draft a follow-up on **Union Collective**.
